curriculum.subject: Physics (Code No. 042)
curriculum.overallDescription: 5 units, 6 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand the need for measurement and systems of units including SI fundamental and derived units.
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis and apply it to physical problems.
Define frame of reference and describe motion in a straight line.
Distinguish between scalar and vector quantities and perform vector addition/subtraction/multiplication.
Explain intuitive concepts of force, inertia, and Newton's three laws.
Calculate work done by constant and variable forces.

components.description: Two experiments one from each section (Section A and Section B)
Record of experiment and activities performed by students
One activity from any section (Section A or Section B)
Report of the project carried out by students
Oral examination on experiments, activities and project

section_a_experiments_list: 1. To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body and to measure internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using Vernier Callipers.
2. To measure diameter of a given wire and thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
3. To determine volume of an irregular lamina using screw gauge.
4. To determine radius of curvature of a given spherical surface by a spherometer.
5. To determine the mass of two different objects using a beam balance.
6. To find the weight of a given body using parallelogram law of vectors.

project_assessment: Requirement: Report of the project carried out by students must be submitted at the time of annual examination.

competency_groups.title: Physical World and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to units, measurements, significant figures, uncertainty determination, dimensional analysis, and dimensions of physical quantities.
competencies: Understand the need for measurement and systems of units including SI fundamental and derived units.
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis and apply it to physical problems.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit I: Physical World and Measurements

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Straight Line
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to frame of reference, motion description using calculus concepts, velocity-time graphs, and uniformly accelerated motion.
competencies: Define frame of reference and describe motion in a straight line.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Plane
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to vector operations, resolution of vectors, and motion in two dimensions including projectile and circular motion.
competencies: Distinguish between scalar and vector quantities and perform vector addition/subtraction/multiplication.

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Newton's laws, momentum conservation, friction types, and dynamics of circular motion.
competencies: Explain intuitive concepts of force, inertia, and Newton's three laws.

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to work done by forces, kinetic/potential energy, conservation laws, collisions, and power.
competencies: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit IV: Work, Energy and Power

competency_groups.title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to center of mass, torque, angular momentum conservation, rotational dynamics, moment of inertia, and equilibrium.
competencies: Determine the center of mass for systems and rigid bodies.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit V: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Kepler's laws, universal gravitation, variation of gravity, gravitational potential/energy, orbital mechanics, and escape speed.
competencies: Apply Kepler's laws and the universal law of gravitation.

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Solids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to elasticity, stress-strain relationships, Hooke's law, moduli (Young's, Bulk, Shear), Poisson's ratio, and elastic energy.
competencies: Analyze the relationship between stress and strain.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Fluids
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to fluid pressure, Pascal's law, viscosity, Stokes' law, Bernoulli's theorem, surface tension, and capillary action.
competencies: Explain the principles behind hydraulic lifts/brakes using Pascal's law.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter

competency_groups.title: Thermal Properties of Matter
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to heat, temperature, expansion, specific heats, calorimetry, latent heat, and modes of heat transfer including radiation laws.
competencies: Differentiate between conduction, convection, and radiation.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to thermal equilibrium, laws of thermodynamics (zeroth through second), state variables, and processes like isothermal/adiabatic.
competencies: Apply the first law of thermodynamics.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory of Gases
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the equation of state, kinetic theory assumptions, pressure concept, temperature interpretation, degrees of freedom, and mean free path.
competencies: Explain the kinetic theory basis for gas pressure.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit IX: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to periodic motion, simple harmonic motion (SHM), energy in SHM, and the simple pendulum.
competencies: Derive expressions for time period of a loaded spring.

competency_groups.title: Waves
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to wave types, speed, superposition principle, reflection, standing waves (strings/pipes), and beats.
competencies: Explain the principle of superposition of waves.

lesson_planning_intelligence.unit_name: Unit I: Physical World and Measurements
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Vernier Callipers, Screw Gauge usage for measurement uncertainty.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Significant figures rules and dimensional analysis problems.

lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Graph plotting for velocity-time and displacement-time.

lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Newton's laws application in friction and circular motion.

lesson_planning_intelligence.unit_name: Unit IV: Work, Energy and Power
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Work done by variable forces and collision problems.
